source.note: Factory sample bundle — geometry/surface area (maps to live lesson 10-3).

card.title: Surface Area of Rectangular Prisms
card.skillFocus: Find the surface area of a rectangular prism by adding the areas of its faces (using a net).
card.objective: I can find the surface area of a rectangular prism.
card.languageObjective: I can explain my work using the words net, face, and surface area.
card.description: Use a net and the formula SA = 2(lw + lh + wh) to find the surface area of rectangular prisms and cubes.

lesson.overview: Students unfold a rectangular prism into a net, see its six faces, and add the face areas to find total surface area, then use the formula SA = 2(lw + lh + wh).
lesson.objective: I can find the surface area of a rectangular prism.
lesson.languageObjective: I can explain my work using the words net, face, and surface area.
materials: Optional: a small box to unfold
lesson.warmUp: A rectangle is 4 cm by 3 cm. What is its area?

vocabulary.definition: A flat side of a solid figure.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un lado plano de una figura sólida.

vocabulary.definition: A flat pattern that folds into a solid.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un patrón plano que se dobla para formar un sólido.
vocabulary.example: Unfold a box and it lies flat as a net

vocabulary.definition: The total area of all the faces of a solid.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El área total de todas las caras de un sólido.
vocabulary.example: Add the area of all 6 faces

vocabulary.definition: A box-shaped solid with 6 rectangular faces.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un sólido en forma de caja con 6 caras rectangulares.

formulas: SA = 2(lw + lh + wh)  — add the three different face areas, then double

modeledExamples.prompt: Find the surface area of a prism with length 4, width 3, height 2 units.
steps: Find the three face areas: lw = 4×3 = 12, lh = 4×2 = 8, wh = 3×2 = 6.

modeledExamples.prompt: Find the surface area of a cube with side 5 units.

practice.prompt: Prism: length 2, width 2, height 3. Find the surface area.
Cube with side 4. Find the surface area.
Prism: length 5, width 3, height 2. Find the surface area.
Prism: length 6, width 4, height 1. Find the surface area.
A cube-shaped box has side 3. Find the surface area.
Prism: length 10, width 2, height 5. Find the surface area.

exitTicket.prompt: Find the surface area of a cube with side 2.
Find the surface area of a prism 2 by 1 by 1.

lesson.reflection: Why do we multiply by 2 in the formula SA = 2(lw + lh + wh)? Explain using the word faces.

misconceptions.misconception: Forgetting to double, so only 3 faces are counted.
misconceptions.fix: Every face has a matching opposite face, so multiply the three areas by 2.

misconceptions.misconception: Finding volume (l×w×h) instead of surface area.
misconceptions.fix: Surface area adds the face areas; volume fills the inside. Different question, different work.

esolSupports: Draw and label the net's 6 faces before adding
Sentence frame: 'The surface area is ___ because I added ___ faces.'
spedSupports: Provide the labeled net for problems 1–2
Give the three products and have students add and double
lesson.extension: Find a real box at home, measure it, and find its surface area.
lesson.subNotes: Self-running: read both examples aloud, then students complete the practice. Answer key included.
